value: Global interest in Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) and fusion prototypes is increasing as governments pursue low-carbon dispatchable power and advanced R&D programs.
notes: High-level program summaries from IAEA and US DOE

value: IAEA provides technical guidance, milestones and member-state support for SMR deployment.
notes: IAEA SMR topic pages and factsheets

value: US DOE Office of Nuclear Energy supports SMR development (funding, regulatory engagement, demonstration support).
notes: DOE program pages and funding notices

value: NuScale Power's SMR design remains a leading commercial SMR candidate with regulatory and deployment activity in the US and export markets.
notes: Company press releases and regulatory filings

value: Rolls-Royce SMR consortium advancing a UK-based SMR program with target deployment timelines and industrial supply-chain initiatives.
notes: Consortium releases and UK government program pages

value: Republic of Korea research and industrial initiatives (KAERI, KHNP, Doosan 등) active on SMART/SMR technologies.
notes: National R&D and corporate program pages

value: ITER and international fusion programs continue construction and commissioning milestones toward demonstration of net energy and fusion research.
notes: ITER official updates

value: Private fusion firms (Commonwealth Fusion Systems, TAE, General Fusion, Helion, etc.) are advancing pilot reactors and funding rounds; monitor for demo milestones.
notes: Company announcements and investor briefings

value: Regulatory frameworks adapt for SMRs: NRC (US), ONR (UK), and national regulators publish design/licensing guidance and engagement pathways.
notes: Regulatory guidance pages

value: SMR supply chain includes reactor modules, fuel fabrication, advanced manufacturing, turbomachinery and grid integration components; industrialization depends on scale and standardization.
notes: Industry reports and prime vendor capability pages

value: up to 300 MWe per module; >80 designs; 4 advanced construction
notes: Topic page; general definitions and global counts
